sanskrit:
रूपं महत्ते बहुवक्त्रनेत्रं महाबाहो बहुबाहूरुपादम्।
बहूदरं बहुदंष्ट्राकरालं दृष्ट्वा लोकाः प्रव्यथितास्तथाहम्॥
transliteration:
rūpaṃ mahat te bahu-vaktra-netraṃ mahā-bāho bahu-bāhūru-pādam |
bahūdaraṃ bahu-daṃṣṭrā-karālaṃ dṛṣṭvā lokāḥ pravyathitās tathāham ||
literal_meaning: O mighty-armed one, seeing Your great form—many mouths, eyes, arms, thighs, feet, bellies, terrible teeth—all the worlds are shaken, and so am I.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna confronts the overwhelming totality of existence, a cosmic form that shatters his familiar ego-boundaries and evokes primal dread; his reaction mirrors the universal human terror when faced with the uncontainable magnitude of life and death.
psychological_state: Acute dissociative awe: the ego’s defensive structures collapse under the archetypal tremendum, producing a full-body existential tremor that is both sacred and traumatizing.
trigger_scenario: Standing before an ICU monitor showing a loved one’s crashing vitals, suddenly grasping the vast machinery of life and death that operates beyond any human control.
anchor_text: Face life’s vastness; let awe dissolve the ego’s walls.
